column5: Sentence
column6: SentenceTranslation

column5: 別府の温泉は昔から、別府に住んでいる人や旅行客に愛されて来ました。
column6: Beppu's hot springs have long been loved by the people who live in Beppu and by travelers.
column7: 昔から = since long ago; for a long time.

column5: 今の建物は1938年に建てられました。
column6: The current building was built in 1938.
column7: Passive past form: 建てられました = was built.

column5: 別府の温泉は、量も種類も多くて、日本一と言われています。
column6: Beppu's hot springs are said to be the best in Japan both in quantity and in variety.

column5: 別府の温泉は、量も種類も多くて、日本一と言われています。
column6: Beppu's hot springs are said to be the best in Japan both in quantity and in variety.

column4: is said to; is reputed to
column5: 別府の温泉は、量も種類も多くて、日本一と言われています。
column6: Beppu's hot springs are said to be the best in Japan because they are abundant and varied.
column7: Polite present form of the common reported-expression chunk と言われている.

column5: 海を見ながら、水着で入れます。
column6: You can get in wearing a swimsuit while looking out at the sea.

column5: 別府には色々なタイプの温泉があります。
column6: In Beppu, there are many different kinds of hot springs.

column5: 泥の温泉です。
column6: It's a muddy hot spring.

column5: 泥に入ると、肌が綺麗になります。
column6: When you soak in the mud bath, your skin becomes beautiful.

column5: これは砂湯です。砂の温泉です。
column6: This is a sand bath. It's a hot spring made of sand.

column5: 砂は少し重いですが、体がとても暖かくなって、気持ちがいいです。
column6: The sand is a little heavy, but your body gets very warm, and it feels great.

column5: 温泉の熱で体を温めます。
column6: The heat from the hot spring warms the body.

column5: 鉄輪むし湯では、服を着たまま、薬の草、薬草の上に寝ます。
column6: At Kannawa Steam Bath, you lie down on top of medicinal grass—medicinal herbs—while still wearing your clothes.
column7: Here, 草 refers to herbs/plants rather than lawn grass.

column5: 鉄輪むし湯では、服を着たまま、薬の草、薬草の上に寝ます。
column6: At Kannawa Steam Bath, you lie down on top of medicinal herbs while still wearing your clothes.
column7: 薬の草 in the sentence restates 薬草 in simpler words.

column5: 薬草の下から温泉の温かい湯気が出てきます。
column6: Warm steam from the hot spring comes up from beneath the medicinal herbs.
column7: Refers to visible steam rising from the hot spring.

column4: to come out; to emerge; to come up
column5: 薬草の下から温泉の温かい湯気が出てきます。
column6: Warm steam from the hot spring comes up from beneath the medicinal herbs.
column7:
出る = something goes out
出てくる = something comes out toward the speaker / comes out into view
てくる is also used to show the change of the state. for example: 太陽が雲の間から出て来ました。

column5: 足湯に入りながらおしゃべりするのも楽しいです。
column6: It's also fun to chat while soaking your feet in a footbath.
column7: Sentence uses the hiragana spelling おしゃべりする.

column4: to put in; to place into
column5: 手だけ温泉の中に入れます。
column6: You can put just your hands into the hot spring.
column7: Transitive counterpart of 入る; here 手を入れる = put one's hands in.

column5: 少し茶色い温泉、白い温泉、青い温泉。
column6: There are slightly brown hot springs, white hot springs, and blue hot springs.

column5: 温泉は色々な病気を治すと言われています。
column6: Hot springs are said to cure all kinds of illnesses.
column7: 色々な病気 = all kinds of illnesses.

column5: 温泉は色々な病気を治すと言われています。
column6: Hot springs are said to cure all kinds of illnesses.
column7: Transitive verb; compare 治る = to get better.

column5: 温泉はストレスも解消してくれます。
column6: Hot springs help relieve stress too.
column7: Common katakana loanword for mental or emotional stress.

column5: 温泉はストレスも解消してくれます。
column6: Hot springs help relieve stress too.
column7: Often used for resolving or relieving things like stress, shortages, or dissatisfaction.

column4: a person you met; someone you became acquainted with
column5: 心も体も綺麗にして、温泉で知り合った人とお喋りするのも楽しいかもしれません。
column6: It might also be fun to clean both your mind and body, then chat with people you met at the hot spring.
column7:
知 (し) り合 (あ) う = to get to know each other
知 (し) り合 (あ) った = got acquainted / met
知 (し) り合 (あ) った人 (ひと) = a person you met / a person you became acquainted with
